_comment: Codex-only hook definitions. Codex <= 0.130.x runs hooks via cmd.exe /C on Windows and $SHELL -lc on unix, and (unlike newer builds) does NOT honor a commandWindows field. So each command is a sh/cmd polyglot with NO double quotes: cmd.exe runs the leading `py %PLUGIN_ROOT%...` branch; POSIX sh skips the `py`/`%VAR%` branches (py absent or path mangled, both nonzero) and runs the `$PLUGIN_ROOT` branch. The codex dispatcher always exits 0 (decision via stdout), so the `||` chain stops at the first branch that launches python. Claude Code uses the separate hooks/hooks.json bash shim.

hooks.command: py %PLUGIN_ROOT%\scripts\codex\_dispatch.py pretooluse || python %PLUGIN_ROOT%\scripts\codex\_dispatch.py pretooluse || python3 $PLUGIN_ROOT/scripts/codex/_dispatch.py pretooluse || python $PLUGIN_ROOT/scripts/codex/_dispatch.py pretooluse

py %PLUGIN_ROOT%\scripts\codex\_dispatch.py posttooluse || python %PLUGIN_ROOT%\scripts\codex\_dispatch.py posttooluse || python3 $PLUGIN_ROOT/scripts/codex/_dispatch.py posttooluse || python $PLUGIN_ROOT/scripts/codex/_dispatch.py posttooluse

py %PLUGIN_ROOT%\scripts\codex\_dispatch.py sessionstart || python %PLUGIN_ROOT%\scripts\codex\_dispatch.py sessionstart || python3 $PLUGIN_ROOT/scripts/codex/_dispatch.py sessionstart || python $PLUGIN_ROOT/scripts/codex/_dispatch.py sessionstart
